idea: a self-hosted read-it-later app with fast full-text search and tagging

product.name: Readpile
product.problem: People save articles to read later but never find them again; hosted services lock data in and die.
users: small teams sharing a knowledge base
product.valueProp: Own your reading queue: a private, fast, searchable archive of everything you save.

goals: A user can save and reliably re-find any article within seconds
Reach 1,000 self-hosted installs in the first year

modules.name: Capture
modules.description: Getting content into the archive: saving, extraction, imports.

featureWishlist.title: Save an article from a URL or browser extension
featureWishlist.notes: Extract clean readable content and store it offline, so that the saved copy renders without the original site being reachable.

featureWishlist.title: Full-text search across all saved articles
featureWishlist.notes: Sub-second search with typo tolerance, so that a reader re-finds any saved article in under 1 second.

featureWishlist.title: Tag and organize saved articles
featureWishlist.notes: Tags are freeform and user-owned, so that a user re-finds any article by tag in one search.

featureWishlist.title: Import an existing Pocket or Instapaper export
featureWishlist.notes: Parse the official export formats, so that an import never loses an article's original save date or tags.

featureWishlist.title: Read articles offline on mobile
featureWishlist.notes: Cache saved articles locally, so that previously synced articles open without a network connection.
